summary: Moved the DamageIceJump-related block out of asm/ft_08A4.s into ftCo_DamageIce.c and partially decompiled the surrounding ftCommon ice-damage code. The PR fully matched small callbacks for DamageIceJump animation, IASA, physics, and collision, provided a C implementation for ftCo_80091854 with supporting type/header discoveries, kept difficult routines behind MUST_MATCH inline asm stubs, and improved report_score.py to print an aggregate file score. The report shows ftCo_DamageIceJump_Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll at 100%, while larger helpers ftCo_800914A4, ftCo_80091620, ftCo_80091854, and ftCo_80091030 remained partial or guarded.
what_changed: Removed roughly the ftCo_800914A4 through ftCo_DamageIceJump_Coll assembly range from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and added corresponding code or guarded inline asm in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce.c.
Made .L_803B74BC a named global symbol ftCo_803B74BC and exposed it as an extern Vec3 const in ftCo_DamageIce.h.

Added C versions for ftCo_DamageIceJump_Anim, ftCo_DamageIceJump_IASA, ftCo_DamageIceJump_Phys, and ftCo_DamageIceJump_Coll. The animation callback decrements fp->mv.co.damageicejump.x0 and calls ft_800CC730 when it reaches zero; physics delegates to ft_80084EEC; collision delegates to ft_800831CC(gobj, ft_80096CC8, ft_80082B1C); IASA is empty.

Added a partial C implementation of ftCo_80091854 that handles ft_800C5240/ftCo_800C5A98, clears fp->x2227_flag.bits.b5, changes to motion state 0x146, plays effect/sound-related calls, sets self velocity from stick/co_attrs fields, and initializes damageicejump.x0 from p_ftCommonData->x7A4.
Kept ftCo_800914A4 and ftCo_80091620 as MUST_MATCH inline asm with NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks for non-matching builds, preserving progress without claiming full C matches.
Changed several existing DamageIce functions to use ftCo_GObj* instead of HSD_GObj* where the file already treats the object as a fighter common GObj.
Added ftCo_DamageIceJumpVars to ftCommon_MotionVars with a float at fp+2340, matching the timer field used by DamageIceJump_Anim.
Corrected inferred field types: p_ftCommonData->x7A4 changed from int to float, and ftCo_DatAttrs x140 at fp+268 changed from int to float.
Typed ftCo_800C5A98 as void ftCo_800C5A98(ftCo_GObj* gobj) in ft_08A4.h.
Added an m2c stack struct for ftCo_80091854 documenting sp24 and a Vec3 at stack offset 0x28.
Updated tools/report_score.py with calc_percent and aggregate file-current/file-max scoring, adding a **File** row to reports.
smart_moves: Used a staged migration strategy: move the asm range into the C translation unit, fully decompile simple callbacks, and fence hard routines with MUST_MATCH inline asm plus NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks rather than blocking all progress on large helpers.
Named the local zero vector as ftCo_803B74BC before using it from C, avoiding anonymous asm-local labels in source-level code.
Introduced a motion-vars substruct for damageicejump.x0 instead of continuing to access fp+2340 as a raw offset in the matched C callback.
Adjusted field types based on float load/store evidence from the disassembly, especially p_ftCommonData->x7A4 and co_attrs.x140.

Kept exact assembly available for ftCo_800914A4 and ftCo_80091620 under MUST_MATCH, preserving matchability while allowing the file to compile in other configurations with explicit NOT_IMPLEMENTED markers.
Improved the score-reporting tool at the same time as the decomp work so future PR bodies can show both per-function and whole-file progress.
decomp_lessons: For ftCommon action callbacks, prefer ftCo_GObj* once the function body immediately treats gobj->user_data as ftCo_Fighter; this reduces casting ambiguity and helps signatures align across headers.
When an fp+2340 scratch slot is reused by a specific motion state, model it in ftCommon_MotionVars with an action-specific struct rather than leaving every use as a raw offset.
Float accesses in asm are strong evidence for correcting unknown int fields to float in shared data structs; this PR changed p_ftCommonData->x7A4 and ftCo_DatAttrs.x140 accordingly.

Small action callbacks are good first targets in a partially matched file: empty IASA, simple physics/collision forwarding, and timer-based anim callbacks can often reach 100% while larger transition/setup helpers remain partial.
If a C implementation still needs exact stack layout, capture inferred stack structs in headers under the existing m2c convention, including offsets and total size.
Converting anonymous asm data labels into named symbols can unlock C references to shared constants without duplicating data.
naming_conventions: Common fighter action callbacks use ftCo_DamageIceJump_Anim, ftCo_DamageIceJump_IASA, ftCo_DamageIceJump_Phys, and ftCo_DamageIceJump_Coll.
Unidentified helper functions retain address-style names such as ftCo_800914A4, ftCo_80091620, and ftCo_80091854.
Anonymous asm data label .L_803B74BC was promoted to a project-style symbol ftCo_803B74BC.
Motion-state local vars were named under union ftCommon_MotionVars as damageicejump with member x0 at fp+2340.
Inferred m2c stack layout uses struct _m2c_stack_ftCo_80091854 with offset comments and /* inferred */ annotations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a contiguous assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s but preserved exact asm for difficult functions inside ftCo_DamageIce.c under #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP).
Used #pragma push/#pragma pop and clang-format off around inline asm to keep assembly stable.
Provided NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallbacks for guarded asm functions in non-MUST_MATCH builds, making partial migration explicit.
For ftCo_DamageIceJump_Anim, matched the asm pattern of comparing a float timer at fp+2340 against 0.0, subtracting 1.0, and calling ft_800CC730 once the timer becomes non-positive.
For ftCo_DamageIceJump_Coll, collapsed the assembly sequence loading ft_80096CC8 and ft_80082B1C into a direct call ft_800831CC(gobj, ft_80096CC8, ft_80082B1C).
The report in the PR body shows remaining nonzero scores for ftCo_800914A4, ftCo_80091620, ftCo_80091854, and ftCo_80091030, so these were not fully matched C despite the PR title focus.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there is no reviewer discussion, no reviewer intent or requested follow-up can be inferred from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile ftCo_800914A4 or ftCo_80091620 after this partial inline-asm migration.
Search for later renames or field refinements for ftCo_DamageIceJumpVars.x0, p_ftCommonData->x7A4, and ftCo_DatAttrs.x140.
Check whether ftCo_803B74BC remains a zero Vec3 constant or is later renamed to a semantic effect offset.
Look for later improvements to report_score.py file-level scoring or report format.
